title: Salesforce permission set and admin setup backend-first contract
salesforce_visible_capabilities: Salesforce permission sets and permission set assignments

existing_domain_foundation_properties: PERMISSION_EVALUATE_ROUTE is /permissions/evaluate and is package-level control evidence, not a mounted Client/Matter admin route
simulatePermissionReadOnly returns read_only true, writes_product_state false, and production_ready_claim false
simulateAdminPermission returns simulator_only true, grant_applied false, and can_grant_access false
evaluatePermission decision order stays fail-closed with deny before allow and object ACL before allow rule
current_ui_boundary: Client/Matter UI may show allow, denied, review_required, and approval_required states from tested route responses
Client/Matter UI may not show permission-set creation, permission assignment, object manager editing, connected app administration, or grant/revoke controls before mounted admin route tests exist
Existing HRX policy consoles are not Salesforce Client/Matter permission-set parity
Existing permission simulator support is read-only evidence and does not apply grants

route_policy_contract.service_boundary: Read tenant-scoped permission set metadata and safe rule summaries only; no principal secrets, raw policy JSON, or cross-tenant counts
Create owner-approved permission set metadata with idempotency and audit evidence; deny broad wildcard actions unless owner policy allows them
Patch label, description, disabled state, and allowlisted rule refs only; every change requires audit and cannot silently broaden access
Read safe assignment metadata for tenant-scoped actors and groups; direct user identifiers are reduced to actor refs and labels
Assign permission set to actor/group only after admin policy, owner decision, idempotency key, and audit intent are present
Revoke permission assignment with dual-control policy when required and append immutable audit evidence
Read object metadata and permission policy refs for allowlisted Client/Matter objects only; schema mutation is out of scope
Patch field policy labels, visibility, and permission refs only after owner-approved metadata policy; no physical schema writes
Read connected app descriptors and disabled/enabled state only; credentials, OAuth secrets, and provider tokens are omitted
Disable connected app descriptor with owner-approved provider policy and audit receipt; provider revocation remains external-provider gated

safe_output_rules: direct principal identifiers omitted unless actor display policy allows them
wildcard actions require explicit owner-approved policy
grant and revoke write audit events
break-glass assignments require reason, approval, and audit intent
simulator output cannot be reused as grant evidence
object manager cannot mutate physical database schema in this contract
Client/Matter field visibility changes require owner policy
privilege labels, ethical walls, legal holds, and raw storage pointers cannot be exposed through generic field policy
new connected apps are disabled by default
provider revocation requires external receipt before trust claims

owner_decisions_required: admin product owner for Client/Matter permission-set setup
who may create, patch, assign, and revoke permission sets
whether assignment writes require dual control
object and field policy allowlist for Client, Contact, Matter, Billing, Analytics, and DMS objects
break-glass approval workflow and retention policy
admin audit retention and review cadence
external_provider_dependencies: None required for first-party permission-set registry and assignment metadata
Identity provider or SSO subject source may be required for production role assignment reconciliation
OAuth or connected-app provider is required before connected-app disable/revocation can claim external effect
verification_cases: VC-SF-B-W06-API-001: list permission sets returns safe metadata and no raw policy JSON
VC-SF-B-W06-API-002: create permission set requires owner policy, idempotency key, and audit intent
VC-SF-B-W06-API-003: patch permission set cannot silently broaden access or add wildcard actions without owner policy
VC-SF-B-W06-API-004: list permission assignments hides direct actor identifiers where policy requires labels
VC-SF-B-W06-API-005: assign permission set requires admin permission, owner policy, idempotency, and audit evidence
VC-SF-B-W06-API-006: revoke assignment emits audit and respects dual-control policy
VC-SF-B-W06-API-007: object manager reads only allowlisted Client/Matter metadata and safe field policy refs
VC-SF-B-W06-API-008: field policy patch cannot mutate physical schema or expose restricted legal/security fields
VC-SF-B-W06-API-009: connected app list omits credentials, secrets, and provider tokens
VC-SF-B-W06-API-010: connected app disable remains provider-gated until external revocation receipt exists
VC-SF-B-W06-UI-001: Client/Matter/Admin UI exposes no permission-set admin, object manager editor, connected-app admin, grant, revoke, or disable controls before route/provider tests pass

required_before_ui_exposure: OMO CodeGraph caller check for permission-set, assignment, object-manager, and connected-app handlers
Hermes MCP check-only: hermes.validate.core and hermes.desktop_authority_boundary
evidence_gate.claim_boundary: Passing this contract validator does not create production, deployment, final approval, or enterprise trust claims.
